references.title: Path-Based Recommender System for Learning Activities Using Knowledge Graphs

extracted.Initialization Method:
The knowledge graphs are built using entity types and relationships.

Latent representations are created using Random-Walk
extracted.Updates to Recommendations: Graph Edges are extended by interactions.
extracted.Preprocessing Details:
Authors include bias parameters p and q to balance local/global walks

- Skipgrams are used to create effiicient node representations
extracted.Comments:
The idea of the authors is to create a new path-based recommender system.

Activities are captured by embeddings, which allows the identification of similar ones.

Provides explainable recommendations via path tracing.

Results on experiment with 100 students show that students who used the recommender have higher satisfaction when it comes to user experience, effectiveness and impact on learning.
extracted.Limitations:
- Single domain.
- No tests against other models.
- Evaluated done only throughout a semester.
- Limited scale - no exploration of the growth of the knowledge graph.

annotations.text: The authors propose a new recommender system for learning activities that uses knowledge graphs. The model uses student-learning activity graph to identify common learning activity pathways.  Authors test their approach in an ITS on the teaching of Java.
